title_en: The Major Battles — Badr, Uhud, and the Trench
content_en:
The military history of the Medinan period centers on three major confrontations with the Quraysh that collectively shaped the destiny of early Islam: the Battle of Badr (2 AH / 624 CE), the Battle of Uhud (3 AH / 625 CE), and the Battle of the Trench (Khandaq, 5 AH / 627 CE). Al-Mubarakpuri gives each battle careful historiographical treatment, examining causes, events, outcomes, and the Quranic commentary that accompanied each.

The Battle of Badr began as an interception of a Meccan trade caravan led by Abu Sufyan, but escalated when Abu Sufyan alerted Mecca and a Qurayshi army of approximately one thousand men marched to confront the Muslims. The Muslim force numbered approximately 313 men — poorly armed and facing professional warriors from Arabia's most powerful tribe. The Quran describes the Muslim position plainly: 'And already had Allah given you victory at Badr while you were few in number' (Quran 3:123). After a day of fighting in which seventy Qurayshi leaders were killed and seventy taken prisoner — including key opponents of Islam — the Muslim force achieved what the Islamic tradition regards as a miraculous victory. The Quran explicitly attributes this to divine intervention: 'And you did not kill them, but it was Allah who killed them' (Quran 8:17). Badr established the military credibility of the new Muslim state and was a decisive blow to Qurayshi prestige.

The Battle of Uhud, the following year, saw the Quraysh return with three thousand men seeking revenge. Initial Muslim success was reversed when the fifty archers the Prophet ﷺ had stationed on a hillside — with explicit orders not to move regardless of what happened — abandoned their positions to collect the battle spoils, believing victory was assured. A Qurayshi cavalry charge through the now-unguarded flank turned the tide. The Prophet ﷺ himself was wounded, a rumor of his death caused temporary chaos among the Muslims, and seventy Companions were martyred. The Quran's response to Uhud is a sustained meditation on the theology of trial: 'And what struck you on the day the two armies met was by permission of Allah' (Quran 3:166). Uhud became the great school of patience, accountability, and correction for the Muslim community.

The Battle of Khandaq (the Trench) represented the Quraysh's most dangerous response — a coalition of approximately ten thousand men from multiple Arabian tribes assembled with the explicit purpose of delivering a final, crushing blow to Medina. The Persian-descended Companion Salman al-Farisi suggested an innovative response borrowed from Persian military strategy: digging a trench (khandaq) along the exposed northern front of Medina, where cavalry could not cross. The trench was dug in approximately two weeks of intense communal effort, with the Prophet ﷺ participating personally. The siege lasted twenty-seven days but ultimately failed — the coalition army was unable to cross the trench, internal divisions were sown through diplomacy, and a fierce windstorm sent by Allah disrupted the besiegers' camp. They withdrew, and the Quran declared: 'And Allah repelled those who disbelieved in their rage; they did not gain any good. And sufficient was Allah for the believers in battle' (Quran 33:25). After Khandaq, the Prophet ﷺ declared: 'Now we will raid them, and they will not raid us.'
title_id: Pertempuran-Pertempuran Besar — Badr, Uhud, dan Al-Ahzab
content_id:
Sejarah militer periode Madinah berpusat pada tiga konfrontasi besar dengan Qurasiy yang secara kolektif membentuk nasib Islam awal: Perang Badr (2 H / 624 M), Perang Uhud (3 H / 625 M), dan Perang Al-Ahzab/Khandaq (5 H / 627 M).

Perang Badr: 313 orang Muslim menghadapi pasukan Qurasiy yang berjumlah sekitar 1.000 orang. Ini adalah pertempuran yang menentukan — kemenangan Muslim pertama yang menetapkan bahwa komunitas baru ini tidak bisa dibasmi. Al-Qur'an menyebutnya sebagai "Yawm al-Furqan" — Hari Pemisahan antara kebenaran dan kebatilan. Nabi ﷺ berdoa dengan penuh kerendahan: "Ya Allah, jika Engkau membinasakan pasukan ini, tidak akan ada lagi yang menyembah-Mu di bumi."

Perang Uhud: Qurasiy kembali dengan kekuatan yang lebih besar. Kaum Muslim sempat hampir menang, namun sebagian pemanah meninggalkan pos mereka untuk mengambil ghanimah, memungkinkan pasukan berkuda Qurasiy untuk menyerang dari belakang. Nabi ﷺ terluka parah dan rumor kematian beliau menyebar. Tujuh puluh sahabat syahid, termasuk Hamzah bin Abdul Muthallib (ra), paman Nabi ﷺ.

Perang Khandaq: Koalisi 10.000 orang dari berbagai suku Arab mengepung Madinah. Salman al-Farisi (ra) mengusulkan strategi revolusioner: menggali parit di sekeliling Madinah — strategi yang tidak dikenal bangsa Arab sebelumnya. Setelah 27 hari pengepungan tanpa hasil, Allah mengirim angin badai yang meporak-porandakan kemah-kemah koalisi dan mereka terpaksa mundur.
content_ar:
يتمحور التاريخ العسكري للمرحلة المدنية حول ثلاثة مواجهات كبرى مع قريش شكّلت مجتمعةً مصير الإسلام الأول: غزوة بدر (2هـ / 624م)، وغزوة أُحد (3هـ / 625م)، وغزوة الخندق (5هـ / 627م). ويتعامل المباركفوري مع هذه المعارك الثلاث بتحليل عسكري وبُعد روحي معاً.

في بدر: تقاتل ثلاثة عشر مؤمناً ضد جيش قريش المدجّج، فانتهت بنصر مُفاجئ جعل يوم بدر في القرآن الكريم «يوم الفرقان». وقُتل من زعماء قريش سبعة وسبعون وأُسر مثلهم. ويُبيّن المباركفوري كيف أن بدراً رسّخت المكانة الدولية للدولة الإسلامية الوليدة.

وفي أُحد بدأ المسلمون بأفضلية تكتيكية ثم أصابتهم النكسة حين غادر الرماة مواضعهم طمعاً في الغنيمة فكشفوا الظهر. وكانت الهزيمة جرحاً مؤلماً لكنها درساً بليغاً، وفي القرآن الكريم تعليق مستفيض على أسبابها وحكمها.

وفي الخندق واجه المسلمون تحالفاً قريشياً غير مسبوق، فأشار سلمان الفارسي بحفر الخندق حول المدينة، ففشل الحصار وانفضّ التحالف بعد قرابة شهر.
